diffCoverage.notes: Checked that persisted history carries no credential/token data, SQL is parameter-bound, and the restart smoke prints only non-secret counts.

findings.evidence: Message bodies are stored as plaintext in SQLite. These are user data on the user's own machine, not a bearer token; ADR-0005 accepts this for durability and the credential triple is encrypted at rest under the keychain master key. Recorded as the accepted known risk message-plaintext-at-rest.
findings.fix: Track SQLCipher / whole-DB encryption as a future ADR; non-blocking.

findings.evidence: All message/thread/attachment SQL uses positional parameter binding (no string interpolation), so persisted message bodies and ids cannot cause SQL injection. The history DTOs (StoredThread/StoredMessage) carry no credential fields, and the restart smoke prints only non-secret counts (2 threads / 3 messages).

summary: Security review (reconciliation): persisted history contains user message data only — no credential or token values — and all store SQL is parameter-bound. The plaintext-message-at-rest posture is the ADR-0005-accepted known risk (credentials remain encrypted). The reconciliation smoke leaks nothing. No blocking findings.
